name: ruby-metrics
title: ruby-metrics
description:
application metrics and instrumentation
Capture metrics about code execution in a vendor agnostic way. As the author
of many libraries which would benefit from metrics, there are few key
priorities: (1) zero overhead if metrics are disabled, minimal overhead if
enabled, and (2) a small and opinionated interface with standardised
semantics.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-metrics because application metrics and instrumentation.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-metrics as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-metrics with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-metrics --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
